name: Constellation Energy Corporation
summary: Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG, Utilities) — composite stance CONSTRUCTIVE. Latest reported quarter FY2026 Q1: revenue $11.12B (+63.8% YoY), net margin 5.2%. Valuation 28.0x forward P/E (+44% vs peers). Spot $93.43 (above 200-DMA).
bull: Top-line momentum — revenue grew +63.8% YoY in FY2026 Q1 to $11.12B, with TTM revenue of $30.58B confirming the run-rate. Growth at this pace is unusual for a company of this scale and reflects either share gains, pricing power, or new-product cycles depending on the line-item mix in the Fundamental tab.
Margin trajectory — net margin expanded +2.5pp over the last 4 quarters in the dataset. Management's cost-out commentary on recent calls is showing up in the P&L, not just the press release.

Trend regime constructive — spot $93.43 sits +33.6% above the 200-DMA ($69.93). The long-term moving average has been a reliable reference for institutional capital and remains supportive of the directional bias.

Sell-side target points higher — consensus PT of $355 (vs spot $93.43) implies +280.0% upside. Cross-checked against the peer-median PE FY1 of 19.5x, the discount to fair value looks underwritten by both buy-side and sell-side.
bear: Margin pressure — net margin of 5.2% in the latest quarter is below the 10% benchmark that gates discretionary capital deployment for most large-caps. Either pricing power is eroding or input costs are running ahead of pass-through.

Valuation premium — 28.0x forward P/E vs peer median of 19.5x — a 44% premium. The setup requires continued out-execution to maintain; any reversion to peer-median multiples compresses the stock meaningfully even before any earnings revision.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 67% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Net margin expands above 6.7% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 61% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Net margin compresses below 3.2% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $49.02 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line: CONSTRUCTIVE. Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) printed $11.12B in revenue in FY2026 Q1 (+63.8% YoY), net margin of 5.2%. At 28.0x forward P/E, the stock carries a 44% premium to peers (19.5x median) — the multiple needs continued execution to defend.Technical regime is constructive: spot $93.43 above the 200-DMA ($69.93, +33.6%) with neutral momentum (RSI 47). Sell-side consensus PT of $355 implies +280.0% upside. The options market is pricing a ±6.1% move by the front-month expiry — consistent with historical realized vol. 30-day implied move is ±13.4%.
